summary: Frame-accurate video player: research (4 angles) confirmed <video>.currentTime unfixable (audio-clock + PTS truncation). Adopt mediabunny hybrid.
Planned; implementation queued.
beginImplementation: maturity planned->in-progress; derived 6-step checklist; created validation.md/checklist.md/files.json.
Derived 6 checklist items from plan.md steps.

Step 1 PASS: mediabunny 1.50.8 installed (zero runtime deps); smoke via Playwright Chromium on real combined_003.mp4 → canDecode=true, 20/20 distinct frame hashes, exact 24fps/0.833s, frame0≠frame1. Added canDecode() cross-platform fallback gate to plan.

Step 2 PASS: js/services/frameSink.js created + verified in LIVE app. UrlSource on real /project-file?path= works (range 206). 20/20 distinct frames via service. Cross-platform fallback proven live: bogus/undecodable url -> canDecode false -> getFrameCanvas returns null (native <video> fallback), never throws.

Step 3 auto-PASS (live app): MpiVideoSurface canvas overlay + frameSink-backed frameStep. Painted frames == sink ground-truth (1/2/3 + step-back), frame0->1 MOVES (bug fixed), canvas shows on step + hides on play. ESLint clean. user-ux eyeball pending in real viewer.
Step 3 USER-VERIFIED in live app (1.2.0): frame-step exact + distinct every press, frame0->1 moves, play hides canvas. Core stuck-playhead bug gone.

Step 4 auto-PASS: sub-range loop freeze fixed in MpiVideoControlBar. _handleRangeBoundary + _seekRangeStartIfNeeded now compare native time vs out-point directly (half-frame lead) + re-assert _play() on wrap; _effectiveFps stub deleted. Loop ON: 6 clean wraps bounded [in,out]. Loop OFF: stops at out no overshoot. ESLint clean. user-ux pending.

Step 3 post-eyeball fixes: overlay canvas sizing bug fixed (width/height:100%+object-fit:contain matches video; rectsMatch verified in wide stage). frame0=frame1 investigated = Wan/LTX padded dup first frame (MAD 0-1=1.25 vs ~9 others), NOT a player bug.

Step 4 in!=0 loop freeze FIXED: removed the t<_in-lead re-seek guard from _handleRangeBoundary (fired every tick after wrap-seek landed a hair below _in, pinning playhead on in-handle). Playback only cares about out-point (time moves forward); play-start in-seek handled by _seekRangeStartIfNeeded. Verified in=10 out=30: 4 clean wraps, no pin, bounded.

Color shift under investigation: sink frame R+6/G+6/B+0 vs <video> capture. User comparing source vs crop-export (no-crop free export = our ffmpeg pipeline) in DaVinci as ground truth to locate the shift (decode-side vs ffmpeg-export-side). Research agent running on mediabunny/WebCodecs color handling.

Color shift LOCATED (DaVinci): crop-export (our ffmpeg pipeline) == source, NO shift. So ffmpeg/export is clean; extend-from-last-frame drift is NOT export. Shift is DECODE-SIDE ONLY: mediabunny CanvasSink (WebCodecs) renders more vivid than true frame; <video> element is correct. Fix target = make frameSink canvas color-match <video> (range/matrix). Awaiting research agent.

Color shift ROOT CAUSE: decoded VideoFrame reports CORRECT color (bt709/bt709/bt709 primaries/transfer/matrix, fullRange:false = limited). Shift is in Chromium drawImage(VideoFrame)->2D-canvas step: it expands limited-range to full-range RGB (sink vivid), while <video>/DaVinci render limited-range as-is (correct). Both technically valid but disagree; <video> is our reference. CanvasSink has NO color option. Fix = correct range in draw. Research agent still finding exact minimal remedy.

Color fix approach: range expansion is INSIDE WebCodecs decode (all 4 draw paths incl copyTo-RGBA give identical vivid pixels; VideoFrame pixels already full-range). Not a draw-trick fix. Simple luma-range compress (219/255) fits R only, not G/B - transform is per-pixel YUV-space, mean-fitting over-corrects. Need proper matrix from research agent. User chose: fix in frameSink. HOLDING for research.

Color fix ANALYSIS: per-channel pixel fit sink->video = R(a0.976,b-2.3) G(a1.016,b-7.4) B(a1.0,b0,resid0). Blue IDENTICAL, R/G diverge by different slopes = YUV MATRIX mismatch (BT.601 vs BT.709), NOT a range op. Confirms research bug 40539111. copyTo-RGBA disproven (same vivid pixels). EXACT fix = self-convert YUV->RGB with correct bt709 limited matrix from copyTo I420 planes. Per-channel RGB approx = resid3/maxDev15 (imperfect). Scope escalation - flagged to user.

COLOR SOLVED: source is BT.601-encoded (libx264 SD-size default) but WebCodecs mis-tags+decodes as BT.709. bt601_limited manual YUV->RGB matches <video> exactly (delta [0.1,-0.05,-0.29] ~zero). <video> uses correct 601. FIX = frameSink self-converts YUV->RGB with matrix picked by resolution heuristic (SD<720 ->601, HD>=720 ->709, same as libx264/<video>). Per-frame cost only, length-independent. copyTo native I420 planes. Need HD-clip confirm for 709 branch.

COLOR FIX SHIPPED (auto-verified): frameSink now VideoSampleSink + manual YUV(I420)->RGB, matrix by height (>=720 BT.709 else BT.601 limited). Matches <video> pixel-near-perfect: SD resid0.12/max1, HD resid0.59/max8. ESLint clean. Frames still exact+distinct. user-ux eyeball pending, then HANDOFF.

COLOR USER-VERIFIED (reloaded): overlay matches video, survives upscale. Extend-from-last-frame color drift EXPLAINED (was extracting frame + WebCodecs recoloring it). Follow-up card needed: apply same BT.601/709 matrix fix to the extend/last-frame EXTRACTION path.

Scrub-back visual glitch (interpolated-flash) FIX attempt: frameStep now paints canvas BEFORE moving video.currentTime (native seek under canvas was flashing its drifted target). Reorder shipped, frames still exact (regression pass). NEEDS user re-eyeball next session.

msg: Step 5 done (auto-verified): unified TrimBar to frame-index mapping (added _frameCount+setFrameCount) matching control-bar _displayTime; fixed double-normalization in _displayTime (now returns true frame time idx/effFps, single normalization in _pctOf); un-stubbed MpiVideoSurface._effectiveFps to fc/dur for Wan last-frame reach. invariant.mjs: 5 clip shapes drop==echo every frame, first@0% last@100%. Awaiting user eyeball.

Step 6 done: docs/video-player.md written (97 lines) + README row. No dead seek-math to remove. Corrected extraction premise: captureFrameBlob draws native <video> = color-correct, NOT WebCodecs; real gap is frame-accuracy. Created MPI-287 (todo). .claude/rules doc-drift pending user OK.

reason: Steps 5-6 done; user-verified 100% accurate; MPI-287 follow-up created.
